steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence needs a finite main verb for the subject “emulsifiers such as cetyl hydroxyethylcellulose” (plural) to complete the independent clause before the infinitive phrase “to blend oil and water into homogeneous formulations.”

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'are employed': A finite plural verb in the passive voice. It agrees with the plural subject “emulsifiers” and serves as the main verb of the independent clause. ✓
  • Choice B — 'employed': A bare past participle. Without an auxiliary verb, it cannot function as the main verb of an independent clause. ✗
  • Choice C — 'that they employ': Introduces a relative clause, leaving the sentence without a main verb and creating a fragment. ✗
  • Choice D — 'being employed': A present participle. Non-finite; cannot serve as the main verb of an independent clause. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
